# cmdbuf.io

> Command buffers: Go libraries for automation. A command is an
> io.Reader (reading it runs it), files and directories are byte
> streams, and one Machine interface runs commands on the local
> system, over SSH, and in containers. Packages: lesiw.io/command
> and lesiw.io/fs.

Every example in these documents compiles and runs against the
current releases.

## Docs

- [Field guide](https://cmdbuf.io/README.md): condensed, example-first
  reference written for coding agents — mental model, shell-to-Go
  translation table, common mistakes, API quick reference
- [Website](https://cmdbuf.io/): concepts with worked examples
- [API reference: command](https://pkg.go.dev/lesiw.io/command)
- [API reference: fs](https://pkg.go.dev/lesiw.io/fs)
- [Source](https://github.com/lesiw/command)

## Mirrors

- [Site mirror](https://cmdbuf.github.io/): same content, hosted on
  GitHub Pages for restricted networks
- [Guide mirror](https://cmdbuf.github.io/README.md)
